texts.full_text: an important characteristic in the design of certain critical components. Generally for metallic alloys, toughness is inversely related to strength, i.e. the higher the strength, the the toughness. Within this relationship, individual alloys and families of alloys display distinctive relationships between strength and toughness. These characteristics can be clearly seen in FIG. 1. Precipitation hardening (PH) stainless steels, as a group, tend to be found in the less desirable, low strength, low toughness portion of this figure. It is particularly challenging for precipitation-hardening (PH) stainless steels like 13-8Mo to achieve both high strength and good fracture toughness simultaneously. Conventional 13-8Mo PH stainless steel has been widely used in applications requiring a combination of corrosion resistance, moderate strength, and some level of ductility; however, its use is often limited by the compromise between these properties. The present invention addresses this issue by providing an composition for 13-8Mo steels that enhances fracture toughness (K.sub.IC) without significantly sacrificing other desirable mechanical or physical properties. This improvement allows the material to be used in more demanding applications where high strength and excellent resistance to crack propagation are both critical, such as aerospace components, medical devices, and structural parts exposed to harsh environments. The improved 13-8Mo steel of this invention achieves its enhanced fracture toughness through a combination of compositional adjustments and optimized heat treatment processes. Specifically: 1. Compositional Adjustments: The alloy composition is fine-tuned by controlling the levels of key elements such as carbon, nitrogen, nickel, chromium, molybdenum, and possibly other minor alloying additions like niobium or titanium. His performance this year has undoubtedly put him at the forefront of discussions regarding the prestigious Golden Glove Award, an accolade that recognizes the best defensive player at each position in both leagues. Zimmerman's dedication to excellence on the field is evident not only through his individual statistics but also by his leadership and team-first attitude. His teammates often speak highly of his work ethic, commitment to improving his game, and willingness to mentor younger players. This combination of skill, determination, and camaraderie has made him a cornerstone of the Washington Nationals organization since he was drafted in 2005. Off the field, Zimmerman remains an active participant in various community initiatives through the team's charitable efforts. texts.full_text: y role of immobilizing prey. Sphingomyelinase D (SMase D) and homologs in the SicTox gene family are the most abundantly expressed toxic protein in venoms of Loxosceles and Sicarius spiders (Sicariidae). While SMase D is well known to cause dermonecrotic lesions in mammals, little work has investigated the bioactivity of this enzyme in its presumed natural role of immobilizing insect prey. We expressed and purified recombinant SMase D from Loxosceles arizonica (Laz-SMase D) and compared its enzymatic and insecticidal activity to that of crude venom. SMase D enzymatic activities of purified protein and crude venom were comparable, indicating the enzyme's activity is not significantly influenced by other components in the venom when isolated. Insect bioassays revealed that Laz-SMase D exhibits potent toxicity against insects, with an LD50 (lethal dose for 50% of test subjects) similar to or even lower than that observed for crude Loxosceles arizonica venom. This suggests SMase D is a major contributor to the insecticidal activity in sicariid spider venoms. Further investigation into the mechanism by which Laz-SMase D exerts its toxicity on insec

texts.full_text: Mexico City to address urban air pollution through its participation in international networks, specifically focusing on the Pan American Air Pollution Sampling Network. Established during a period marked by rapid industrial growth and motor vehicle proliferation, this network facilitated data exchange among North, Central America, South America countries aiming at understanding regional atmospheric conditions better. Keywords: Urban Environmental History; Mexico City; International Cooperation; Transboundary Issues The late 20th century witnessed significant urbanization across Latin American cities. Among them was the rapidly expanding metropolis of Mexico City (MCD), where rapid industrial growth and an influx of rural migrants led to severe air pollution issues by mid-1967, prompting urgent actions from local authorities. In response, MDC joined international efforts such as those initiated under UNESCO's auspices in 1950s aimed at addressing global environmental challenges. The Pan American Air Pollution Sampling Network (PAN) was one of these initiatives established to monitor and study air quality across the Americas systematically over time by collecting standardized atmospheric data. Mexico City’s participation allowed it not only access but also contributed valuable local insights into regional pollution dynamics, influencing policy decisions both domestically as well internationally regarding urban environmental management. This article delves deeper into how this collaboration shaped MCD's approach to tackling its pressing air quality concerns within a broader context of international scientific cooperation during the Cold War era. --- This text provides an introduction based on available details and extrapolates potential content for Sabrina Kirschner’s scholarly wo

texts.full_text: Revive Your Resume for New Opportunities Revive, by definition, is to restore from an inactive or unused state; to become active or flourishing again. After weeks or months of submitting your resume to a countless number of job postings with little or no response, the job search process can become very frustrating and discouraging. Maybe it’s time to consider reviving your resume and bringing it back to life. The current job market is fast-paced and competitive. To give yourself a fair shot and an impressive edge over your competition, a focused and comprehensive resume is essential. It’s your personal marketing tool that will show the prospective employer what you can bring to the table. Below are several tips on how you can bring your current resume back to life and start getting noticed by employers. Ways to Revive Your Resume Lead with the Important Information The top third of your resume should display a Career Summary which effectively presents your skills, qualifications, achievements, competencies and areas of expertise as they pertain to the targeted position. When employers open your document, you want to lead with your strongest information and entice them to continue reading. All bullet pointed job duties should also be organized in order of importance. texts.full_text: copper recovery process from waste printed circuit boards using Fe³⁺/Fe²⁺ redox system. The present study aimed at developing an original and environmentally friendly process for the recovery of copper from waste printed circuit boards (WPCBs) by chemical dissolution with Fe(3+) combined with the simultaneous electrowinning of copper and oxidant regeneration. The recovery of copper was achieved in an original set-up consisting of a three chamber electrochemical reactor (ER) connected in series with a chemical reactor (CR) equipped with a perforated rotating drum. Several experiments were performed in order to identify the optimal flow rate for the dissolution of copper in the CR and to ensure the lowest energy consumption for copper electrodeposition in the ER. The optimal hydrodynamic conditions were found to be 0.05 m/s for the stirring speed in the CR and 20 m/h for the flow rate in the ER. Under these conditions, a copper extraction efficiency of 97% was achieved from WPCBs with an energy consumption of 1.2 kWh/kg Cu. The obtained copper powder was of high purity (99.8%) and could be directly used in several industrial applications. This original process thus appears as a promising alternative to conventional methods for copper recovery from WPCBs, offering both economic and environmental benefits.

The books published by Forest Dhamma Books cover a wide range of topics related to Buddhist philosophy, meditation practices, and ethical living based on the principles laid down in the original teachings of Siddhartha Gautama, also known as the Buddha. texts.full_text: in optical read/write heads of optical recording and reproduction devices are known, for example, from European Patent Application 0 199 565 A3. Each of the components described therein involves integration in a silicon wafer of two photodetectors, namely a signal detector for receiving optical signals reflected from a CD and a monitor detector for monitoring a power output of an associated semiconductor laser chip. The two photodetectors are disposed one behind the other as seen in the radiating direction of the semiconductor laser chip and lie on a beam axis of a laser beam emitted by the semiconductor laser chip. A semitransparent routing mirror disposed above the signal detector allows a small part of the radiation emitted by the semiconductor laser chip through to the monitor detector and deflects a larger part of the laser radiation away from the silicon wafer by reflecting it through 90.degree.. The reflected laser radiation reaches the CD, is reflected back to the semiconductor laser component according to the reflection pattern on the CD, passes through the semitransparent routing mirror and strikes the signal detector disposed beneath it. However, that configuration has two disadvantages. Firstly, a laser radiation coming from the semiconductor chip and scattered by the semitransparent routing mirror strikes the signal detector directly and interferes with the optical signal reflected from the optical data storage medium. Secondly, the fabrication of semitransparent mirrors with characteristics adequate for that application is very difficult. A known proposal exists for combining an opto-magnetic tracking with a magnetic read/write head of a magnetic storage device. That proposal includes fixing next to each other on the mechanically moved carrier arm of the read/write head, the laser source as a discrete component in the form of a semiconductor laser chip in a metallic TO packing (diameter approximately 5 mm), and the necessary beam shape and beam control optics and

texts.full_text: It began like three days before the outreach for our team to meet with a pastor in California who had been disfellowshipped because he did not agree that women should be ordained as pastors or elders within his church denomination, yet they are equal in God’s eyes and haVe every right to do so. It was an interesting time listening to this man of God talk about the theology behind it all while we got a glimpse into how our faith has shaped us from different perspectives; that Jesus Christ is not just Lord but Savior too! He spoke passionately on what he believed – “Jesus saves!” In light of his testimony, I want you know there are many things which can happen during these trips if one does nothing else except open their hearts and minds to be challenged by God’s Word. This pastor reminded me that we must always remember who Jesus really is: our Lord! We cannot let anything distract us from Him or His teachings; He alone gives life eternal (John 3:16). I have been blessed beyond measure during this trip – I know without doubt it will continue to bless my soul for years ahead. It has given me a new perspective on how we can serve God in every way possible, including through worshiping Him together as one body! There is no greater joy than knowing you are part of something bigger than yourself; serving others while glorifying Jesus Christ along the journey toward heaven makes life worth living each day here upon earth. If anyone wants to hear more about what happened during these past few weeks please feel free contact me anytime at henryimwumbe@gmail.com or call/text 509-218-7643 if you would like an update on my trip so far! I am happy answer any questions regarding the theology behind it all, as well as give advice and guidance for those who may be interested in serving God through worshiping Him together. texts.full_text: bound to K-ras versus H/N- ras. Peptides corresponding closely (but not completely identically due the insertions of Ala residues at positions where the two conformations are quite different as judged by their distance variation matrices from molecular dynamics calculations over 10 nsec) were synthesized and evaluated for activity in blocking signal transduction via Ras signaling using a human tumor cell line A43 I. All four peptides strongly blocked both proliferation/survival (by MTS assay with doses of the protein synthesis inhibitor cycloheximide that normally completely suppresses this effect, but can be rescued by oncogenic activation such as N-ras overexpression) and transformation via matrix invasion assays in these cells after 48 hours. Moreover all four peptides bound K− Ras directly; although three had very similar affinities within an order of magnitude or so while one (corresponding to residues 960–1,257 ) was markedly weaker despite being indistinguishable from other sequences by primary structure comparison . This observation highlights how critical even small differences can be for modulating biological activity – something that must always considered when designing inhibitors based on molecular dynamics simulations especially given such phenomena like induced

texts.full_text: ention has been paid to the level of dioxins in breast milk in Japan, but few large-scale studies have been conducted on the subject. From 1997 to 2002, we collected 839 samples of breast milk from primiparas residing throughout Japan. Starting in 1999, breast milk was also collected from secundiparas. Seven isomers of polychlorinated dibenzo-p-dioxins (PCDDs), 10 of polychlorinated dibenzofurans (PCDFs), 4 of coplanar polychlorinated biphenyls (PCBs), and 11 non-coplanar PCBs were analyzed using high-resolution gas chromatography coupled with high-resolution mass spectrometry (HRGC/HRMS). The mean total toxic equivalent (TEQ) concentration of dioxins in breast milk was 3.4 pg WHO-TEQ/g lipid, with significant regional variations observed. The highest concentrations were detected in samples from the western and central regions of Japan, potentially influenced by historical industrial activities and dietary habits (e.g., fish consumption). Secundiparas exhibited slightly higher TEQ levels compared to primiparas, possibly reflecting the bioaccumulation of dioxins over time. The study also identified correlations between maternal age, dietary patterns, and dioxin concentrations. Notably, consumption of marine foods was associated with elevated levels of certain PCB congeners. These findings underscore the importance of regional and lifestyle factors in dioxin exposure and highlight the need

texts.full_text: s world because of vaccinations. Another example of clever manipulation of the immune system is the story of a pneumonia vaccine. In a 2003 study, researches noted there had been a huge drop in hospitalizations of the elderly for pneumonia, with 12,000 fewer yearly deaths, especially in those older than 85. This is the result of routine childhood pneumonia vaccination. Although we now encourage two different pneumonia vaccines for thoswe who need them most — adults over age fifty-five and seniors livingin assisted care facilities—this vaccine remains effective if administered as recommended during early life. Just like our children, influenza virus has evolved several times in recent decades into new strains which do not cause full immunity from earlier infections or vaccinations. But we keep trying to improve vaccination systems by using computer modelling of the proteins involved with each type and strain; flu vaccines are available for most adults every year. There is even an app-based version so that a physician can identify if there were enough antibody markers left over in your blood after you had already been vaccinated. The poliovirus vaccine was developed early on to halt epidemics which caused thousands of permanent neurological deformities, many worse than death itself.. The iron fist campaign by Jonas Salk’s team prevented the worst epidemic since 1840 and halted new paralytic injuries from most all forms this terrible virus. When vaccines are available for these common human viruses they have a very high effectiveness rate. Of course no vaccine is perfect — especially if you know how immunizations work in adults (as opposed to children). texts.full_text: operties. Multiband RF filters with independently controllable passbands are an essential component in dynamic multiband RF communications. Unfortunately, even a fixed multiband RF filter without the capability to adjust the passband properties individually is very difficult to achieve using either RF electronics or microwavephotonics alone. To address this challenge and enable independent tunability of each band within an integrated photonic device while maintaining low insertion loss for all bands, a novel approach has been developed. This involves utilizing cascaded Mach-Zehnder interferometers (MZIs) with phase modulators to create multiple passbands in the microwave frequency range. The key innovation lies at fabricating two sets of MZI structures on separate silicon-on-insulator wafers and subsequently bonding them together, creating a multilayered device. Each set contains independent tunable filters that can be adjusted without affecting other bands' performance. This architecture allows for precise control over the spectral properties such as center frequency (f0), bandwidth Δω) of each passband by varying phase shifts introduced through modulation in different arms within MZI structures. The benefits are twofold: Firstly, it provides independent tunability which is crucial when operating under dynamic conditions. Secondarily and importantly for practical applications such as communication systems or radar sensors where high-frequency signals need to be filtered efficiently with minimal power loss. In addition that the photonic components in this design have inherent advantages over their electronic counterparts like low noise figure, wide bandwidth capability (upwards of 100 GHz), fast tuning speed on picosecond timescales and compatibility for integration into existing RF systems. This novel approach offers a promising solution to address current challenges within multiband communications. It could lead towards the realization of more advanced photonic integrated circuits that can dynamically adapt their filter characteristics in real-time, potentially paving way new applications including satellite communication or cognitive radio networks. texts.full_text: Lagrangian approach offers a novel perspective on the Born-Oppenheimer molecular dynamics framework. By inTroducing extended electronic degrees of freedom as auxiliary variables, the model allows for a more comprehensive description of the system's evolution. The coordinates and momenta follow the standard Born-Oppenheimer potential energy surface, while the auxiliary electronic variables exhibit harmonic oscillator-like behavior around the adiabatic ground state. this generalization facilitates a smoother and more stable dynamical evolution, particularly in regions where the electronic energy levels exhibit significant changes. texts.full_text: Annual (Vol. 4) - Batman: The Dark Knight Returns to Gotham! : Although this was published in the mid-'80s during a different era within DC Comics' continuity timeline adjustments known as Crisis on Infinite Earth, it's worth noting that some stories from earlier years were reprinted or referenced. - Wonder Woman #217: This issue introduced Wonder Girl (Cassandra Sandsmark), who would become an important character in the future of both her own series and other DC titles. However, focusing specifically to 1972: - The introduction/reintroduction/continuation/expansion upon various characters such as Black Lightning by writer Dwayne McDuffie began during this period. His work on Green Lantern/Green Arrow #63 in December of that year was pivotal. Please note: Some events may have been published later but set earlier, or vice versa due to the nature and history rewriting practices within comic book storytelling. - Detective Comics (Vol 1) No.428: This issue introduced Wonder Woman's sister Hades as a villainess in her own right. These are just some examples of what was happening with DC characters during that year, but remember this is based on publication
